name: Lenny White
abstract: Leonard White III (born December 19, 1949) is an American jazz fusion drummer who was a member of the band Return to Forever led by Chick Corea in the 1970s. White has been called "one of the founding fathers of jazz fusion".

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Freddie Hubbard Sextet, the Sonny Rollins Quartet, and the McCoy Tyner Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: Stanley recommended Lenny for the new band, the Hymn of the Seventh Galaxy band... That was the first Return To Forever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: And then Lenny White became the producer, which worked out for me, and we took all those fusion tunes and turned them into R&B.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: You’re talking about the sessions? ... yourself, Lenny White and Don Alias.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: Stanley Clarke, Chick, Lenny White, Bill Connors was on guitar. And it was a spiritual experience.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 7, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: listen to how great Lenny White sounds on Return to Forever and listen to him on Red Clay.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: I was over there playing percussion and it was Lenny White and DeJohnette playing drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002
